threshold_event: Public final-authority hidden-human follow-through for Nate or an equally strong hidden-human-as-AI row
current_state: `LIMEN-C-006` is still accusation-stage only and `QUEUE-HUMAN-AS-AI-001` remains explicit negative-result control.
threshold_trigger: One public judgment, regulator-final order, consent decree, settlement order, or comparably authoritative disposition that supports hidden-human-behind-AI language beyond charge stage.
would_change_now: Would narrow or retire the queue row, strengthen `LIMEN-C-006`, permit a stronger Table 1C posture statement, and materially change the shard-009 limitations block.
would_not_change: Would not justify cross-sector prevalence claims, automatic denominator widening beyond explicit editorial choices, or product-truth claims outside the exact authority text.
paper_thesis_use: Threshold map for the first LIMEN paper, thesis claim-support map, and hostile-reviewer appendix showing which future event would actually change the hidden-human claim ceiling.
dashboard_hook: Replace the queue badge only after the exact authority upgrade is captured and routed with explicit posture text.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one real final-authority document route over another wording-only memo.

threshold_event: Exact caption or docket normalization upgrade for `LIMEN-000005`
current_state: The Nate row already has public DOJ text plus OCR-qualified indictment support, but exact caption/docket metadata remains partly unresolved.
threshold_trigger: Cleaner OCR, a public docket-facing text surface, or another exact public record that normalizes the caption/case-number details without changing posture.
would_change_now: Would strengthen document-depth defensibility, source-anchor precision, and manuscript/dashboard citation hygiene for the existing charge-stage row.
would_not_change: Would not close `QUEUE-HUMAN-AS-AI-001`, would not convert allegations into adjudicated fact, and would not by itself change the four-row Table 1C default ladder.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/results note for thesis and reviewer response explaining that document-depth improvement and authority-posture improvement are different gains.
dashboard_hook: Document-depth badge or tooltip refinement for the Nate row, not a posture-color change.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Reopen only if one exact public record improves caption normalization reproducibly.

threshold_event: Official municipal or equivalent public corroboration for the Sveta Trojica comparator
current_state: `LMWCS-20260606-005` remains a non-official comparator kept outside the authoritative ladder.
threshold_trigger: One direct municipal page, council record, procurement trace, vendor disclosure, or official correction with exact service-description language.
would_change_now: Would upgrade the comparator from methods-only scarcity evidence to a stronger non-finance/public-sector corroboration route and could justify a revised overlay or appendix role.
would_not_change: Would not settle hidden-human authority, would not justify default-ladder promotion without deliberate package review, and would not erase translation/source-authority caution automatically.
paper_thesis_use: Useful for a public-sector AI-theater appendix, route-scarcity methods note, and dashboard overlay logic about non-finance official corroboration.
dashboard_hook: Comparator overlay could become an official-route badge if exact public corroboration lands.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Treat as a secondary threshold after the hidden-human final-authority route.

threshold_event: Deliberate synchronized decision to show DoNotPay inside the default Table 1C ladder
current_state: DoNotPay is already strong enough for shared authoritative-core presence and a diversification sidecar, but the default Table 1C ladder intentionally remains four visible rows plus the queue control.
threshold_trigger: An explicit editorial decision to revise the shared table/caption/manuscript/dashboard bundle together, with subtype caution retained and denominator changes documented across all consumers.
would_change_now: Would change presentation on shared surfaces by turning the current sidecar into a default-visible row and would require synchronized caption, export, manuscript, and dashboard rewrites.
would_not_change: Would not close the hidden-human queue, would not create judicial unauthorized-practice findings, and would not turn one FTC row into cross-sector maturity proof.
paper_thesis_use: Editorial-governance artifact for the paper package and thesis exhibits so later writers know this is a synchronized denominator choice, not an evidence discovery event.
dashboard_hook: Table 1C denominator badge, sidecar-to-core migration note, and reviewer-facing legend update only if the whole bundle is patched together.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the sidecar rule frozen unless a shared-surface revision is truly needed.
